name: ntm
description: Named Tmux Manager - Multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini in tiled tmux panes. Visual dashboards, command palette, context rotation, robot mode API, work assignment, safety system. Go CLI.
capabilities: Spawn and manage multiple AI agents in tmux panes
Send commands to specific agent types or all agents simultaneously
Visual dashboard with agent status, token metrics, and context indicators
Command palette with fuzzy search for pre-configured prompts
Context window rotation to prevent overflow
Robot mode API for machine-readable output and automation
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: NTM is a legitimate multi-agent orchestration tool for coordinating AI coding assistants. Static findings show mostly false positives from documentation examples. The only confirmed pattern is the pipe-to-shell install method, which is standard practice for CLI tools. Users should review the safety system documentation and avoid using dangerous permission bypass flags in agent configurations.
valueStatement: Managing multiple AI coding agents creates window chaos and breaks workflow. NTM solves this by orchestrating Claude, Codex, and Gemini agents in unified tmux sessions with visual dashboards and command palette.
userTitle: Orchestrate Multiple AI Agents in Tmux Sessions
limitations: Requires tmux 3.0 or higher installed on the system
Agents run with user permissions and full filesystem access
No built-in authentication for multi-user environments
Agent outputs may contain sensitive project information

useCases.title: Multi-Agent Code Review
useCases.description: Send code to Claude for analysis, Codex for implementation, and Gemini for testing in parallel

useCases.title: Infrastructure Automation
useCases.description: Coordinate multiple AI agents to design, implement, and document infrastructure changes together

useCases.title: Agent Comparison Studies
useCases.description: Run identical prompts across different AI models to compare outputs and approaches systematically

promptTemplates.title: Basic Multi-Agent Setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new project with AI assistance
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a tmux session with 2 Claude agents for architecture and 1 Codex agent for implementation

promptTemplates.title: Parallel Code Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Send my authentication module to all agents and ask for security review

promptTemplates.title: Divide and Conquer
promptTemplates.prompt: Have Claude design the API, Codex implement it, and Gemini write tests

promptTemplates.title: Competitive Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Ask all agents to implement a rate limiter and compare their solutions side by side

bestPractices: Always review agent outputs before implementing code suggestions
Use context rotation features to prevent token overflow in long sessions
Install the safety system to block dangerous commands like git force push
antiPatterns: Sending sensitive credentials or API keys to AI agents
Allowing unrestricted webhook notifications without reviewing endpoints
Running agents with dangerous permission bypass flags like --dangerously-skip-permissions

faq.question: Is NTM compatible with all tmux versions?
faq.answer: NTM requires tmux 3.0 or higher for full functionality and features

faq.question: What are the system requirements?
faq.answer: Go 1.19 or higher, tmux 3.0+, and a terminal with 256-color support

faq.question: Can I integrate NTM with my existing workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, NTM provides shell integration and command hooks for extensive customization

faq.question: How does NTM handle sensitive data?
faq.answer: NTM has a safety system to block dangerous commands but agents still have full file access

faq.question: What if an agent gets stuck or encounters an error?
faq.answer: Use 'ntm interrupt' to stop all agents or zoom into specific panes to restart them

faq.question: How is NTM different from running agents manually?
faq.answer: NTM provides orchestration, persistence across SSH, and unified management across all agents
